next_step: /cdr:verify against task-1.2.1's acceptance criteria and test spec
acceptance_criteria: Leaf and internal node formats are defined and serialize/deserialize round-trip losslessly
index/name.idx file is created on first use

pointers.OpenIndexFile: func OpenIndexFile(path string) (*os.File, error) -- end of file

pointers.TestNodeSerialization: top-level test func, matches required -run TestNodeSerialization

notes_for_verifier: This implementation intentionally covers only node struct definitions + Encode/Decode + OpenIndexFile, per explicit scope boundary in the task -- no lookup/insert/split/delete/scan logic.

NodeSize fixed at 4096 bytes (mirrors engine/catalog.PageSize); no explicit page-size guidance was found in docs/LLD/btree.md, so this was an implementation judgment call worth double-checking against later subtasks' needs (e.g. fanout in 1.2.3 insert/split).
Key encoding is length-prefixed (uint16 length + raw bytes), sequential -- not a slotted-page/free-list design like engine/catalog/page.go, since there is no delete/reuse concern in this subtask.

LeafNode.NextLeaf sentinel value 0 means 'no sibling', following engine/catalog's convention that ID/page 0 is reserved; worth confirming this doesn't collide with a real node index 0 once 1.2.3's allocator is implemented.
gofmt and go vet were run clean on engine/btree after implementation.
